content_sections.heading: About Us
content_sections.body:
Theatre 17 is a community theatre group based in **Dryden, Ontario**, serving Dryden and the surrounding area.\
Performances are held at **The Centre** or **The Dryden Go-Getters**

content_sections.heading: Mission & Vision
content_sections.body: *"To provide quality theatre entertainment to the community of Dryden and the surrounding area by offering a platform for local performers, stagehands & theatre enthusiasts to participate in producing quality stage shows for community enjoyment."*

content_sections.heading: History
content_sections.body:
The original Theatre 17 was formed in the 1980s by a group of like-minded arts enthusiasts. It ran successfully for several years before disbanding.

In 2005, Judith Hamilton, Susan Andrusco, and Derrick Hiscox came together to resurrect community theatre in Dryden. At their first meeting, the group voted to adopt the name Theatre 17 as a nod to the original group.

Their first production, "Quilters", was presented in February 2006. It was directed by Judith Hamilton and Susan Andrusco, and featured a cast of nine women and two men, telling the story of pioneer hardships through quilt blocks.

On the name "Theatre 17": There is speculation — it may reflect the number of founding members of the original group, or it may be a reference to Highway 17, the road that connects Dryden to the rest of the country.

sidebar_cards.heading: Join Theatre 17
sidebar_cards.body: Performers, crew, and supporters all welcome. Reach out to learn more.

page_header.heading: About Theatre 17
page_header.subheading: Fifty years of live theatre in the heart of Northwestern Ontario.

content_sections.heading: Our Story
content_sections.body:
Theatre 17 was founded in 1972 by a small group of Dryden residents who believed their community deserved live professional-quality theatre — not just as an occasional import, but as a permanent and vital part of local life. Naming themselves after the 17th District of Northwestern Ontario, they put on their first production in a converted church hall with borrowed costumes and hand-painted sets.

Over fifty years later, Theatre 17 has produced more than 140 shows, launched countless careers, and become one of the defining cultural institutions of Dryden. We perform at the Dryden Performing Arts Centre and reach an annual audience in the thousands.

content_sections.heading: What We Believe
content_sections.body:
Theatre 17 is a volunteer community theatre. Every person on stage, behind the curtain, and behind the scenes gives their time freely because they love what live performance does for people — as participants and as audience.

We believe theatre belongs to everyone. We welcome new members at any experience level, and we are committed to producing shows that reflect the breadth of our community — comedies, dramas, musicals, Shakespeare, and original work.

content_sections.heading: Youth Theatre Program
content_sections.body:
In 2003, Theatre 17 established its Youth Theatre Program, offering workshops and performance opportunities to young people aged 10–17 throughout the school year. The program has introduced over 400 young people to the performing arts and produced an annual youth show every spring since 2004.

Youth Theatre registrations and information are available through the [Contact](/contact.html) page.

content_sections.heading: How to Get Involved
content_sections.body:
There are many ways to be part of Theatre 17, and you don't need to want to act. We are always looking for people to help with set construction, costumes, props, lighting, sound, front-of-house, and administration. Auditions are held twice yearly for our spring and fall productions.

Visit the [Auditions & Contact](/contact.html) page to register for upcoming auditions or to introduce yourself to our membership coordinator.

content_sections.heading: Community Partners
content_sections.body: Theatre 17 is grateful for the ongoing support of the Town of Dryden, the Dryden District Community Foundation, the Ontario Arts Council, and our many local business sponsors whose generosity keeps ticket prices accessible to all.
